headline: **MLB Daily Recap: May 5, 2026**
article:
The New York Mets secured a 4-2 win over the Colorado Rockies, with Carson Benge leading the offense and Juan Mejia impressing in relief. The Boston Red Sox edged the Detroit Tigers 5-4, with Payton Tolle earning the win and Jarren Duran leading the Red Sox offense. The Tampa Bay Rays dominated the Toronto Blue Jays 5-1, while the Philadelphia Phillies took down the Miami Marlins in a shutout victory.

Several key players are dealing with injuries, including Drew Thorpe, Jake Cronenworth, Yainer Diaz, Ramón Urías, Josh Smith, Steven Matz, Gabe Speier, and Erik Miller, who have all been placed on the injured list. The Chicago White Sox transferred Drew Thorpe to the 60-day injured list, while the San Diego Padres placed Jake Cronenworth on the 7-day injured list due to concussion symptoms.

The Chicago White Sox acquired Trevor Richards from the Philadelphia Phillies in a trade, while the Atlanta Braves traded Jonah Heim to the Athletics for cash. The Washington Nationals traded Zack Short to the Detroit Tigers for cash, and the Toronto Blue Jays traded Tyler Fitzgerald to the Los Angeles Dodgers for cash.

top_news.title: **Mets Take Down Rockies in 4-2 Victory**
top_news.summary: The New York Mets secured a 4-2 win over the Colorado Rockies, with Carson Benge leading the offense with a home run and one RBI. On the mound, Juan Mejia impressed in relief with 5 strikeouts in 2 innings of work.

top_news.title: **Red Sox Edge Tigers in Thrilling 5-4 Victory**
top_news.summary: The Boston Red Sox secured a narrow win over the Detroit Tigers, with Payton Tolle earning the win after 7.0 innings of scoreless relief. Jarren Duran led the Red Sox offense with a 3-hit, 1-home run performance.

top_news.title: **Rays Shut Down Blue Jays 5-1**
top_news.summary: The Tampa Bay Rays dominated the Toronto Blue Jays, securing a 5-1 victory. Ryan Vilade led the Blue Jays with a home run and three RBIs.

top_news.title: **Phillies Take Down Marlins in Shutout Victory**
top_news.summary: The Philadelphia Phillies secured a 1-0 win over the Miami Marlins, with Aaron Nola pitching 6.0 innings of scoreless baseball. Bryce Harper led the Phillies' offense with a 3-hit, 1-home run performance.

top_news.title: **Brewers' Shutout Bid Falls Short in St. Louis**
top_news.summary: The Milwaukee Brewers were unable to secure a win, as the St. Louis Cardinals took home a 0-0 victory.

top_news.title: **Rockies Shut Down Mets in Scoreless Tie**
top_news.summary: The Colorado Rockies secured a 0-0 victory over the New York Mets, claiming the win in a game where no standout hitters or pitchers were noted.

top_news.title: **White Sox RHP Drew Thorpe Shifts to 60-Day IL**
top_news.summary: Chicago White Sox right-hander Drew Thorpe has been transferred from the 15-day injured list to the 60-day injured list. This move comes as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery.

top_news.title: **White Sox Acquire Trevor Richards in Trade**
top_news.summary: The Chicago White Sox have bolstered their pitching staff with the addition of right-handed pitcher Trevor Richards, acquired in a trade with the Philadelphia Phillies for cash. Richards joins the White Sox roster as the team looks to strengthen its rotation.
